courses.course: Ph.D. in Computer Applied Technology, School of Computer Science

timeline.title: Research Intern
timeline.company: Beijing Innovation Center of Humanoid Robotics
timeline.summary: Designing memory systems and World Action Models for embodied world models, improving long-horizon task planning and reliable generation in robotic scenarios.
highlights: Designed an embodied memory system to reduce temporal inconsistency and memory drift in long-video world model generation.
Built a World Action Model around the memory system to improve long-horizon robot task planning.
Explored how world models can strengthen VLA generalization and task understanding.

timeline.title: Joint Lab (Simplexity Robotics & PKU) Researcher
timeline.summary: Conducted research in the Joint Lab (Simplexity Robotics & PKU) on VLA deployment in real mobile manipulation scenarios, spanning task understanding, action generation, execution feedback, and robot-system deployment.
highlights: Optimized whole-body control and mobile-manipulation coupling for stable action generation under complex motion states.
Explored robot proprioception modeling by integrating joint-torque and other physical-state signals.
Introduced Human-in-the-Loop reinforcement learning with online human feedback to improve real-world adaptability and robustness.
Incorporated multi-task, multi-view human data collected from data gloves into VLA pretraining and downstream training.
Deployed and operated Galaxea R1 Lite and Franka Emika Panda robots end-to-end, covering data collection, cleaning, training, testing, and validation.
Designed and fabricated 3D-printed robot components to support rapid hardware iteration and system debugging.

timeline.title: Research Intern, Embodied Multimodal LLM Research Center
timeline.summary: Contributed to RoboBrain 1.0/2.0 and co-proposed AC-DiT, an end-to-end VLA framework for mobile manipulation with state-of-the-art benchmark performance.
highlights: Co-built RoboBrain 1.0/2.0 to improve LLM/VLM spatial understanding, temporal modeling, and long-chain reasoning.
Co-proposed AC-DiT, an end-to-end VLA framework that achieved SOTA on multiple mobile-manipulation benchmarks.
Handled hardware debugging, real-robot data collection, training, and testing on Mobile ALOHA and compound mobile manipulators.
Built mobile-manipulation datasets and benchmarks across multiple simulators, including VR and SpaceMouse teleoperation pipelines.
Led construction of robot digital assets across simulators, including 100+ objects and 10+ scenes.
